title: elatine hexandra subsp. major
intro: Elatine hexandra subsp. major, also known as water wort or greater waterwort, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the family Elatinaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Elatine hexandra subsp. major include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat conditions such as diarrhea, fever, and respiratory infections.
